description_long: This is a pair of well-used pliers featuring two green, rubber-coated handles with a diamond-patterned grip. The handles are slightly curved and show signs of wear, including scuffs and discoloration. The metal jaws are robust and darkened from use, with a blunt, flat gripping surface and a central pivot joint. The jaws appear to have a basic serrated pattern for improved grip and may include a wire-cutting notch near the pivot. The overall construction suggests durability, with the handles designed for comfort and slip resistance. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A worn pair of green-handled pliers with rubberized, diamond-patterned grips and sturdy, serrated metal jaws. The tool shows signs of heavy use and has no visible branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing both green handles, the full jaw profile, and the central pivot joint.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the handles and the alignment of the jaws.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handles and the closed jaw tips.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle curvature and the side of the jaws.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, rubber-coated handles with a diamond-patterned, ergonomic grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Blunt, flat, serrated jaws with a possible wire-cutting notch near the pivot; standard for general-purpose pliers.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot joint; does not appear to be adjustable or slip-joint.
attribute_grip_pattern: Diamond-patterned, rubberized texture for enhanced grip and slip resistance.
